commentaries.author: Ambrosiaster
commentaries.content: Because they gave themselves to God wholeheartedly, they wanted to offer even more than their strength allowed. Commentary on Paul’s Epistles.

commentaries.author: Cornelius a Lapide
commentaries.content: For to their power . . . they were willing. Of their own free will, without being solicited, they came forward and contributed as much as and more than they were able to afford.

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: The greatness of the Macedonians can be seen from the fact that they gave voluntarily, in spite of their poverty.

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: For according to their power, I bear witness. Trustworthy is the witness. And beyond their power. That is, it abounded unto the riches of their liberality. Or rather, he makes this plain, not by this expression alone, but also by all that follows; for he says, of their own accord. Lo! Yet another excessiveness.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
